title: AST Runaway Juvenile/Vehicle Theft: Soldotna [AK26027488]
summary: On 3/29/2026 at approximately 2030 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Soldotna received a report of a stolen vehicle, believed to be taken by a 13-year-old female who ran away from home during the preceding few hours. AST received numerous tips and updates from concerned friends and family attempting to assist in locating the juvenile and vehicle. At approximately 0030 hours on 3/30/2026, AST safely secured both the juvenile and vehicle in Anchor Point.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Vehicle Theft 1st, Theft 1st, and resisting arrest: Wasilla [AK26025531]
incident_type: Vehicle Theft 1st, Theft 1st, and resisting arrest
summary: On 03/23/2026 at 0630 hours, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a vehicle theft in progress on Fairview Loop. Investigation revealed, William Ogan 42 YOA unlawfully took an individual’s vehicle and got it suck nearby. Alaska State Troopers with the assistance of Wasilla Police Department and Mat-Su Narcotic Team arrested William on charges of Vehicle Theft 1st, Theft 1st, and Resisting Arrest. William is being held at Mat-Su Pretrial without bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Vehicle Theft 1st, Theft 1st, and resisting arrest

co_agencies: Mat-Su Narcotic Team arrested William on charges of Vehicle Theft 1st

title: AST Warrant Arrest / Vehicle Theft I: Tok [AK26019705]
summary: On 03/05/2026 at approximately 0032 hours, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a vehicle theft from the parking lot of Bears Den Lounge in Tok. Investigation revealed Nancy A. Joe, age 34, was absconding on an active arrest warrant 4TO-25-00082CR with a $250,000 Dollar bail with 10% posting. Further Joe was reported to have taken the vehicle a gray 2003 Hyundai Tiburon bearing AK plate GJV675 without owner’s consent and departed the parking lot. The vehicle was located in the ditch in the Tok area and recovered. Joe was located later in the evening and arrested on the outstanding warrant. The keys belonging to the vehicle were located on Joe’s person and Joe later confessed to taking the vehicle. Joe was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center on the outstanding warrant and charge
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

summary: On January 23, 2026, AST responded to a report of a vehicle theft in the area of West Tanana Drive and Sheep Creek Road in Fairbanks. The investigation is still ongoing.

title: AST Vehicle Theft, False Information: Fairbanks [AK26004139]
summary: On January 14th, 2026, Fairbanks AST received a report that a stolen vehicle had been located at Sunrise Bagel on Geist Rd. Anthony Pitka was located in the vehicle and provided AST with a false name. Anthony was arrested and remanded to FCC for Vehicle Theft I and False Information.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Vehicle Theft 1: Wasilla [AK26003572]
summary: On 1/13/26 at approximately 0353 hours, AST received a report of a vehicle theft in Houston. Investigation revealed that Korie Russell, age 35 of Wasilla, stole a vehicle and drove it to a residence in Wasilla. On 1/14/26 at approximately 0140 hours, AST contacted Korie on a traffic stop in Wasilla. Korie was arrested for multiple outstanding warrants and for Vehicle Theft 1, and remanded to MSPT.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Fraudulent use of Access Device, other Fraudulent ID charges, & MICS 6th: Mile 228 Parks Hwy [AK26000149]
incident_type: Fraudulent use of Access Device, other Fraudulent ID charges, & MICS 6th
summary: On 01/01/2026 AST and Denali National Park Rangers responded to a report of a stolen vehicle and contacted the vehicle near Mile 228 Parks Highway. During the investigation AST contacted Zihao Jin, age 20 from Florida and he was in possession of a fake ID and marijuana. Jin was arrested and remanded to FCC and held without bail pending arraignment for Obtaining an access device by Fraudulent means, Fraudulent use of an ID card, Possession of Fraudulent ID, and MICS 6th. Further investigation determined the occupant of the vehicle made a mistake and took the wrong vehicle from the Airport car lot in Fairbanks. The vehicle was returned to the owner.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Fraudulent use of Access Device, other Fraudulent ID charges, & MICS 6th

title: AST Burglary 1st, Vehicle Theft 1st, Criminal Mischief 5th: Anchor Point [AK25127357]
summary: On 12/24/25 at 1601 hours, Troopers responded to a report of a suspicious vehicle at an address off Dedra Avenue in Anchor Point. Troopers located the vehicle and contacted the three occupants identified as Roen Bice, 24 of Anchor Point, Stacie Counts, 50 of Anchor Point, and Tabitha George, 28 of Anchor Point. An investigation determined the vehicle was stolen from a separate residence in Anchor Point which was found to have been burglarized. All three occupants were taken into custody and remanded into the Homer Jail. Bice was remanded on charges of Vehicle Theft 1st and Burglary 1st. George and Counts were remanded a Criminal Mischief charge for riding in a stolen vehicle. The investigation is ongoing and more charges are possible.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: an address off Dedra Avenue in Anchor Point

title: AST Vehicle Theft: Glennallen [AK25126092]
summary: On December 21, 2025, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of a vehicle theft from Glennallen Towing and Recovery. At approximately 0100 hours, a white Chevrolet Express van entered the property, attached a tow strap to a black Pontiac Grand Prix, and towed the vehicle away. The van was last observed traveling westbound on the Glenn Highway. This case remains under active investigation. If anyone has any information regarding this incident, please contact the Glennallen State Troopers are 907-822-3263. Update----- The white chevy has been located and all occupants interviewed.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Vehicle Theft, Theft 2nd degree, arrest warrant x 2: Wasilla [AK25117334]
incident_type: Vehicle Theft, Theft 2nd degree, arrest warrant x 2
summary: On 12/10/2025 at approximately 0623 hours, AST responded to a report of vehicle that was partially blocking the road on Skyview Dr and Fast Ln in Wasilla. Investigation revealed the vehicle was a 2001 white Dodge Ram 1500 that was reported stolen on 11/22/2025. Jourdan Avila, age 33 of Wasilla was in possession of the stolen vehicle. Jourdan was arrested for vehicle theft 1st degree and Theft 2nd degree. Additionally, Jourdan had two outstanding arrest warrants. Jourdan was transported and remanded to Matsu Pre-trial Facility in Palmer without bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Vehicle Theft, Theft 2nd degree, arrest warrant x 2
